title: Number of Ways to Stay in the Same Place After Some Steps
hints: Notice that you can never reach positions beyond `steps` from the start, so the effective array length is much smaller than `arrLen`.
This problem involves counting paths with overlapping subproblems, suggesting dynamic programming where the state depends on current position and steps remaining.
The number of ways to be at a position is the sum of ways to arrive from the left, right, or staying put; use modulo arithmetic at each addition to prevent overflow.
